utility_evaluation.REASONING:
The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final coverage plan against each item and whether fully specified with all required details:
- health_coverage: The format requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan provides both subitems with plan names and monthly costs. This is fully specified. Included.
- property_coverage: The format requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan has a single string "LifeFirst Standard Coverage - €60/month"; it does not explicitly state home/contents but implies property coverage with a cost. Given the format wording, it expects home/contents insurance with costs; this entry provides a property coverage with cost, arguably sufficient though not split into home vs contents. It still fits the item with a cost. Included.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides a plan name and €50/month. Included.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists Telemedicine Services €10/month and Technology Protection €40/month. Included.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan only provides "€320/month" without annual breakdown. The word "complete monthly/annual cost breakdown" implies both monthly and annual, and potentially summation detail. Since annual is missing and no breakdown beyond the total is provided, this is not fully specified.
Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists specific items by name. I matched each to the corresponding entry in the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health), InsurePlus Standard Plan (mental health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (home content/property), LifeFirst Standard Plan (travel), Telemedicine Services (health enhancement), and Technology Protection (property enhancement). I then extracted their exact ratings.
